> *Excluding transfers and both sides of credit-card payments, your true spending averaged **$7,471.95 per month** across April–June 2026.*

## Monthly spending

| Month | True spending |
|---|---:|
| April 2026 | $10,426.27 |
| May 2026 | $5,312.45 |
| June 2026 | $6,677.14 |
| **3-month average** | **$7,471.95** |

April includes a **$3,850 property-tax payment**. Without that large annual expense, April was **$6,576.27**, and your normalized three-month average becomes approximately **$6,188.62 per month**.

### What was excluded

| Excluded cash movement | Monthly amount |
|---|---:|
| Transfers to Schwab and two 529 accounts | $1,200.00 |
| Credit-card payments | $956.41–$1,485.91 |

I counted purchases when they occurred on the Visa or Amex, then excluded the checking-account payments to those cards so nothing was double-counted.

**Best working monthly figure:** use **about $6,200** for ordinary spending, while separately reserving roughly **$321 per month** for the observed annual property-tax bill. That produces a planning baseline near **$6,510 per month**.

This uses three complete months of transactions from your connected checking account, Visa, and Amex. April’s checking feed is truncated after April 9 for some recurring utility data, so the normalized baseline may be modestly understated.
